name: defense response
description: "Reactions, triggered in response to the presence of a foreign body or the occurrence of an injury, which result in restriction of damage to the organism attacked or prevention/recovery from the infection caused by the attack." [GOC:go_curators]

name: immune system process
description: "Any process involved in the development or functioning of the immune system, an organismal system for calibrated responses to potential internal or invasive threats." [GO_REF:0000022, GOC:add]

name: regulation of immune response
description: "Any process that modulates the frequency, rate or extent of the immune response, the immunological reaction of an organism to an immunogenic stimulus." [GOC:ai]

name: response to external stimulus
description: "Any process that results in a change in state or activity of a cell or an organism (in terms of movement, secretion, enzyme production, gene expression, etc.) as a result of an external stimulus." [GOC:hb]

name: response to stress
description: "Any process that results in a change in state or activity of a cell or an organism (in terms of movement, secretion, enzyme production, gene expression, etc.) as a result of a disturbance in organismal or cellular homeostasis, usually, but not necessarily, exogenous (e.g. temperature, humidity, ionizing radiation)." [GOC:mah]

name: immune response
description: "Any immune system process that functions in the calibrated response of an organism to a potential internal or invasive threat." [GO_REF:0000022, GOC:add]

name: defense response to other organism
description: "Reactions triggered in response to the presence of another organism that act to protect the cell or organism from damage caused by that organism." [GOC:dos]

name: response to other organism
description: "Any process that results in a change in state or activity of a cell or an organism (in terms of movement, secretion, enzyme production, gene expression, etc.) as a result of a stimulus from another living organism." [GOC:ai]

name: innate immune response
description: "Innate immune responses are defense responses mediated by germline encoded components that directly recognize components of potential pathogens." [GO_REF:0000022, GOC:add, GOC:ebc, GOC:mtg_sensu]

name: response to external biotic stimulus
description: "Any process that results in a change in state or activity of a cell or an organism (in terms of movement, secretion, enzyme production, gene expression, etc.) as a result of an external biotic stimulus, an external stimulus caused by, or produced by living things." [GOC:go_curators]

name: regulation of immune system process
description: "Any process that modulates the frequency, rate, or extent of an immune system process." [GOC:add]

name: response to biotic stimulus
description: "Any process that results in a change in state or activity of a cell or an organism (in terms of movement, secretion, enzyme production, gene expression, etc.) as a result of a biotic stimulus, a stimulus caused or produced by a living organism." [GOC:hb]

name: defense response to symbiont
description: "Reactions triggered in response to the presence of a symbiont that act to protect or prevent damage to the host." [GOC:pg]

name: biological process involved in interspecies interaction between organisms
description: "Any process evolved to enable an interaction with an organism of a different species." [GOC:cc]

name: regulation of response to stimulus
description: "Any process that modulates the frequency, rate or extent of a response to a stimulus. Response to stimulus is a change in state or activity of a cell or an organism (in terms of movement, secretion, enzyme production, gene expression, etc.) as a result of a stimulus." [GOC:jid]

name: myeloid leukocyte activation
description: "A change in the morphology or behavior of a myeloid leukocyte resulting from exposure to an activating factor such as a cellular or soluble ligand." [GOC:add, ISBN:0781735149]

name: regulation of defense response
description: "Any process that modulates the frequency, rate or extent of a defense response." [GOC:mah]

name: regulation of response to external stimulus
description: "Any process that modulates the frequency, rate or extent of a response to an external stimulus." [GOC:mah]

name: cell surface
description: "The external part of the cell wall and/or plasma membrane." [GOC:jl, GOC:mtg_sensu, GOC:sm]

name: regulation of response to stress
description: "Any process that modulates the frequency, rate or extent of a response to stress. Response to stress is a change in state or activity of a cell or an organism (in terms of movement, secretion, enzyme production, gene expression, etc.) as a result of a disturbance in organismal or cellular homeostasis, usually, but not necessarily, exogenous (e.g. temperature, humidity, ionizing radiation)." [GOC:dhl]

name: leukocyte activation
description: "A change in morphology and behavior of a leukocyte resulting from exposure to a specific antigen, mitogen, cytokine, cellular ligand, or soluble factor." [GOC:add]

name: binding
description: "The selective, non-covalent, often stoichiometric, interaction of a molecule with one or more specific sites on another molecule." [GOC:ceb, GOC:mah, ISBN:0198506732]

name: positive regulation of response to external stimulus
description: "Any process that activates, maintains or increases the rate of a response to an external stimulus." [GOC:mah]

name: cell activation
description: "A multicellular organismal process by which exposure to an activating factor such as a cellular or soluble ligand results in a change in the morphology or behavior of a cell." [GOC:mgi_curators]

name: mast cell activation
description: "The change in morphology and behavior of a mast cell resulting from exposure to a cytokine, chemokine, soluble factor, or to (at least in mammals) an antigen which the mast cell has specifically bound via IgE bound to Fc-epsilonRI receptors." [GOC:mgi_curators, ISBN:0781735149]

name: positive regulation of immune response
description: "Any process that activates or increases the frequency, rate or extent of the immune response, the immunological reaction of an organism to an immunogenic stimulus." [GOC:ai]

name: positive regulation of immune system process
description: "Any process that activates or increases the frequency, rate, or extent of an immune system process." [GOC:add]

name: positive regulation of defense response
description: "Any process that activates or increases the frequency, rate or extent of a defense response." [GOC:mah]

name: regulation of innate immune response
description: "Any process that modulates the frequency, rate or extent of the innate immune response, the organism's first line of defense against infection." [GOC:ebc]

name: protein binding
description: "Binding to a protein." [GOC:go_curators]

name: positive regulation of biological process
description: "Any process that activates or increases the frequency, rate or extent of a biological process. Biological processes are regulated by many means; examples include the control of gene expression, protein modification or interaction with a protein or substrate molecule." [GOC:jid]

name: leukocyte mediated immunity
description: "Any process involved in the carrying out of an immune response by a leukocyte." [GO_REF:0000022, GOC:add, ISBN:0781735149]

name: regulation of response to biotic stimulus
description: "Any process that modulates the frequency, rate, or extent of a response to biotic stimulus." [GOC:add]

name: immune response-regulating signaling pathway
description: "The cascade of processes by which a signal interacts with a receptor, causing a change in the level or activity of a second messenger or other downstream target, and ultimately leading to the activation, perpetuation, or inhibition of an immune response." [GOC:add, ISBN:0781735149, PMID:15771571]

name: immune effector process
description: "Any process of the immune system that executes a component of an immune response. An effector immune process takes place after its activation." [GO_REF:0000022, GOC:add, ISBN:0781735149]

name: cell surface receptor signaling pathway
description: "The series of molecular signals initiated by an extracellular ligand binding to a receptor located on the cell surface. The pathway ends with regulation of a downstream cellular process, e.g. transcription." [GOC:signaling]

name: activation of immune response
description: "Any process that initiates an immune response." [GO_REF:0000022, GOC:add, ISBN:0781735149]

name: cytoplasm
description: "The contents of a cell excluding the plasma membrane and nucleus, but including other subcellular structures." [ISBN:0198547684]

name: positive regulation of response to biotic stimulus
description: "Any process that activates or increases the frequency, rate, or extent of a response to biotic stimulus." [GOC:add]

name: positive regulation of cellular process
description: "Any process that activates or increases the frequency, rate or extent of a cellular process, any of those that are carried out at the cellular level, but are not necessarily restricted to a single cell. For example, cell communication occurs among more than one cell, but occurs at the cellular level." [GOC:jid]

name: positive regulation of response to stimulus
description: "Any process that activates, maintains or increases the rate of a response to a stimulus. Response to stimulus is a change in state or activity of a cell or an organism (in terms of movement, secretion, enzyme production, gene expression, etc.) as a result of a stimulus." [GOC:jid]

name: cellular anatomical structure
description: "A part of a cellular organism consisting of a material entity with granularity above the level of a protein complex but below that of an anatomical system. Note that cellular organisms exclude viruses." [GOC:kmv]

name: leukocyte activation involved in immune response
description: "A change in morphology and behavior of a leukocyte resulting from exposure to a specific antigen, mitogen, cytokine, cellular ligand, or soluble factor, leading to the initiation or perpetuation of an immune response." [GOC:add, ISBN:0781735149]

name: cellular response to chemical stimulus
description: "Any process that results in a change in state or activity of a cell (in terms of movement, secretion, enzyme production, gene expression, etc.) as a result of a chemical stimulus." [GOC:mah]

name: cell activation involved in immune response
description: "A change in the morphology or behavior of a cell resulting from exposure to an activating factor such as a cellular or soluble ligand, leading to the initiation or perpetuation of an immune response." [GO_REF:0000022, GOC:add, ISBN:0781735149]

name: regulation of leukocyte mediated immunity
description: "Any process that modulates the frequency, rate, or extent of leukocyte mediated immunity." [GOC:add]

name: immune response-activating signaling pathway
description: "The series of molecular signals generated by a ligand binding to its receptor that lead to the activation or perpetuation of an immune response." [GOC:add]

name: response to stimulus
description: "Any process that results in a change in state or activity of a cell or an organism (in terms of movement, secretion, enzyme production, gene expression, etc.) as a result of a stimulus. The process begins with detection of the stimulus and ends with a change in state or activity or the cell or organism." [GOC:ai, GOC:bf]

name: positive regulation of innate immune response
description: "Any process that activates or increases the frequency, rate or extent of the innate immune response, the organism's first line of defense against infection." [GOC:ebc]

name: Il3rb1-Shc complex, IL-3 stimulated
description: Il3rb1-Shc complex, IL-3 stimulated

name: positive regulation of gene expression
description: "Any process that increases the frequency, rate or extent of gene expression. Gene expression is the process in which a gene's coding sequence is converted into a mature gene product (protein or RNA)." [GOC:txnOH-2018]

name: Cholesterol biosynthesis
description: Cholesterol biosynthesis

name: regulation of myeloid leukocyte mediated immunity
description: "Any process that modulates the frequency, rate, or extent of myeloid leukocyte mediated immunity." [GOC:add]

name: inflammatory response
description: "The immediate defensive reaction (by vertebrate tissue) to infection or injury caused by chemical or physical agents. The process is characterized by local vasodilation, extravasation of plasma into intercellular spaces and accumulation of white blood cells and macrophages." [GO_REF:0000022, ISBN:0198506732]

name: nucleoside phosphate metabolic process
description: "The chemical reactions and pathways involving any phosphorylated nucleoside." [GOC:mah]

name: regulation of cytokine production
description: "Any process that modulates the frequency, rate, or extent of production of a cytokine." [GOC:add, ISBN:0781735149]

name: positive regulation of cytokine production
description: "Any process that activates or increases the frequency, rate or extent of production of a cytokine." [GOC:add, ISBN:0781735149]

name: purine-containing compound metabolic process
description: "The chemical reactions and pathways involving a purine-containing compound, i.e. any compound that contains purine or a formal derivative thereof." [GOC:mah]

name: cytokine production
description: "The appearance of a cytokine due to biosynthesis or secretion following a cellular stimulus, resulting in an increase in its intracellular or extracellular levels." [GOC:add, ISBN:0781735149]

name: proteoglycan binding
description: "Binding to a proteoglycan, any glycoprotein in which the carbohydrate units are glycosaminoglycans." [ISBN:0198506732]

name: nucleobase-containing small molecule metabolic process
description: "The cellular chemical reactions and pathways involving a nucleobase-containing small molecule: a nucleobase, a nucleoside, or a nucleotide." [GOC:vw]

name: positive regulation of biosynthetic process
description: "Any process that activates or increases the frequency, rate or extent of the chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the formation of substances." [GOC:go_curators]

name: myeloid leukocyte mediated immunity
description: "Any process involved in the carrying out of an immune response by a myeloid leukocyte." [GO_REF:0000022, GOC:add, ISBN:0781735149]

name: negative regulation of immune system process
description: "Any process that stops, prevents, or reduces the frequency, rate, or extent of an immune system process." [GOC:add]

name: Il3rb1-Shc-Ship complex, IL-3 stimulated
description: Il3rb1-Shc-Ship complex, IL-3 stimulated
